subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the psychological state and behavior of Apoorva in detail
Understand narrative techniques, imagery, suspense, and characterization
Evaluate the interplay between imagination, fear, and perception
Develop advanced inferential reading and analytical writing skills
Connect the story’s lessons to personal, educational, and social contexts
Enhance vocabulary, comprehension, and critical thinking

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Contextual Analysis
topics_covered: Author: Anthony Hope and narrative style
Psychology of fear and human perception
teaching_strategy: Teacher-led discussion on fear and human perception
Board diagram showing character, setting, and context
student_activities: Predict events and conflicts based on chapter title
Discuss real-life experiences with fear and imagination

lesson_plan.title: Close Reading – Character and Initial Conflict
topics_covered: Introduction to mirror and snake imagery
Internal thoughts and initial psychological conflict
teaching_strategy: Guided discussion to analyze behavior and emotional response
student_activities: Extract textual evidence for character traits and fears
Explain Apoorva’s initial reactions in detail

lesson_plan.title: Rising Action – Suspense and Psychological Analysis
topics_covered: Imagery and descriptive language enhancing fear
Analysis of internal dialogue and psychological conflict
teaching_strategy: Teacher-led discussion of narrative and psychological elements
student_activities: Sequence events causing fear with explanations
Discuss psychological factors leading to panic

lesson_plan.title: Climax, Resolution, and Character Insight
topics_covered: Recognition of the snake as a reflection
teaching_strategy: Board flowchart from rising action to resolution
Discussion on emotional and psychological lessons
student_activities: Write a paragraph analyzing the climax and character insight

lesson_plan.title: Themes, Literary Devices, and Inferential Analysis
topics_covered: Themes: Fear, perception, imagination, and human psychology
Literary devices: imagery, narrative style, suspense, and characterization
Inferential and critical analysis of moral and psychological lessons
teaching_strategy: Textual analysis and discussion on literary techniques
Board illustration of themes and devices with examples
student_activities: Identify literary devices and their effects
Write a 6–8 sentence inferential summary highlighting lessons learned
assessment: Quality and depth of inferential summary

lesson_plan.title: Vocabulary, Reflection, and Application
topics_covered: Advanced vocabulary from the chapter and context-based usage
Reflective writing on fear, imagination, and perception
Application of story insights in personal and social life
teaching_strategy: Board explanation of advanced vocabulary with examples
Reflective discussion prompts connecting story to real-life situations
student_activities: List new words with meanings and use them in sentences
Write a reflective paragraph on personal experiences of fear and imagination
Class discussion on applying lessons from the story in real life

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze Apoorva’s psychology deeply, discuss narrative and literary techniques, and connect the story’s lessons to personal, academic, and social experiences.
